killed.reason: Degenerate price proxy invalidates the lead-lag verdict. The permutation null is ROBUST (p=0.034-0.047 across seeds 7/1/42/123/2024, all significant) and the edge is NOT a #46/#47 scorer-change artifact (32/35 points predate the scorer change; score-vs-score_raw divergence on RDDT is negligible, mean +0.0014). BUT the price_proxy series the lead-lag correlates against is degenerate: across the 24 de-bursted points there are only 9 distinct price values (167.0 x7, 166.71 x5, 162.37 x5), 8 of 23 consecutive price-changes are exactly ZERO, and there is a 24.1h capture gap mid-series. The corpus price extractor is returning repeated/stuck boilerplate quotes, so the lead-lag is correlating real sentiment drift against a step-function price artifact, not real price discovery. A permutation test that shuffles sentiment labels cannot detect a corrupted price axis, so p=0.0345 does NOT certify a tradeable edge. CHARTER: data quality is upstream of every verdict; no fabrication, no look-ahead. NOT ARMED.
killed.reopen_criteria: RDDT (or a cross-sectional basket) clears the full gate on a NON-degenerate price series: a real per-capture quote (Robinhood/finance live quote, not corpus-extracted), >=15 distinct price values with <20% zero-return fraction over n_spaced>=24, no multi-hour capture gaps, AND permutation p<=0.10.

payload.thesis: predate sentiment->price lead-lag edge on RDDT (authoritative EDGE, conviction=0.4908)
open_risks: Computer must verify live price, account, sizing caps, kill-switch, and re-confirm the edge has not decayed before promoting.
